value_picks.name: Boston Scientific Corporation

fcf_yield.detail: FCF 2.807B / mcap 69.50B = 4.04%, just inside the 4-7% borderline band.

pe_discount.detail: Fwd PE 12.55 vs implied PE 20.27 (median target 75.50 / fwd EPS 3.725) = 38.1% discount, >30%.

drawdown.detail: Down 57.3% from 52w high of 109.50; well past the 35% threshold.

balance_sheet.detail: Net debt (cash 1.45B - debt 11.03B), but D/E 42.25 (<50) AND current ratio 1.90 (>1.2) satisfies the pass condition.

institutional.detail: Institutions 95.06% (>75%), rec_mean 1.48 (<2.5); 27 of 31 analysts buy/strong-buy, zero sells.

ai_exposure.detail: Medical devices (MedSurg/Cardiovascular). AI offers modest tailwind in diagnostics and procedure planning but does not move the core interventional-device demand curve. Not a disruption risk.

deal_radar.assessment: Short interest more than doubled (28.6M -> 57.5M shares, +100.7% MoM) yet still only 3.87% of float at 2.71 days to cover — a notable build but not a crowded short. Reads as hedging into the drawdown rather than a directional bear thesis.

catalysts_general: Trading at a 38% discount to the implied PE from analyst median target (75.50).
Earnings growth 100% with PEG 0.57 — growth materially under-priced on forward earnings.
Forward PE 12.55 vs trailing 19.56 implies strong EPS expansion (fwd EPS 3.72 vs trailing 2.39).
Gross margin 68.9%, operating margin 20.6%, ROE 14.7% on revenue +11.6%.

downside_scenarios.scenario: Break below 52w low on continued med-device multiple de-rating.
Recession/margin scare compresses to a 10x forward multiple.

ideal_entry.reasoning: Already pennies off the 52w low (45.99); accumulate into the 45-46 zone where forward PE sits near 12x against double-digit growth and an analyst consensus 60%+ above spot.

comparables.note: Not in input; larger, slower-growing med-device peer trades at a premium PE to BSX.
Not in input; closest growth-quality peer, markedly more expensive than BSX's 12.5x.

value_picks.analyst_take: Best risk/reward in the group: a high-margin, double-digit-grower de-rated to 12.5x forward after a 57% drawdown, with 27 of 31 analysts bullish and a median target ~60% above spot. The only blemishes are a thin 4% FCF yield and no dividend. Score 5.0/6.

value_picks.name: Workday, Inc.

fcf_yield.detail: FCF 3.119B / mcap 32.01B = 9.74%, clears the 7% bar comfortably.

pe_discount.detail: Fwd PE 10.25 vs implied PE 12.86 (median target 162.50 / fwd EPS 12.64) = 20.2% discount, in the 15-30% band.

balance_sheet.detail: Net cash: cash 4.35B > debt 3.80B (+0.55B). Pass on net-cash condition despite current ratio 1.01.

institutional.detail: Institutions 107.2% (>75%, >100% reflects shorting), rec_mean 2.00 (<2.5); 23 of 41 buy, no sells.

ai_exposure.detail: Enterprise HCM/Financials SaaS. AI agents can be embedded into Workday's workflows (tailwind), but agentic automation of back-office headcount is a structural threat to seat-based pricing. The two roughly offset — watch net seat trends.

deal_radar.assessment: Elevated short interest at 15.44% of float (5.06 days to cover) — by far the highest short load in this group — but flat MoM (+3.5%). A crowded but stable short; any beat could squeeze given the cover ratio.

catalysts_general: Forward PE collapsed to 10.25 from trailing 40.37 — large implied EPS step-up (fwd EPS 12.64 vs trailing 3.21).
Earnings growth 248%, PEG 0.51 — cheapest growth-adjusted name alongside the discount.
9.7% FCF yield is the highest in the group; gross margin 75.8%.
Net-cash balance sheet (4.35B cash) gives downside cushion.

downside_scenarios.scenario: Retest of 52w low (110.36) on a soft bookings print.
Broad SaaS de-rating drags the forward multiple under 8x.

ideal_entry.reasoning: Buy into the 110-115 base near the 52w low where the stock holds support (SMA50 ~128 above) and the forward multiple is single digits against a 9.7% FCF yield and net cash.

comparables.note: Not in input; larger enterprise-SaaS peer trades at roughly double WDAY's forward multiple.
Not in input; direct ERP/HCM competitor at a meaningful premium.

value_picks.analyst_take: Cheapest name on FCF yield (9.7%) and growth-adjusted value (PEG 0.51), with a net-cash sheet and 10x forward earnings. The 15% short float is the swing factor — it amplifies both squeeze upside and any guidance disappointment. Score 5.0/6, a high-conviction value-growth setup.

value_picks.name: Intuit Inc.

fcf_yield.detail: FCF 5.232B / mcap 77.07B = 6.79%, top of the 4-7% borderline band (just under 7%).

pe_discount.detail: Fwd PE 10.31 vs implied PE 16.83 (median target 460 / fwd EPS 27.34) = 38.7% discount, >30%.

drawdown.detail: Down 65.4% from 52w high of 813.70 — deepest drawdown in the group.

balance_sheet.detail: Roughly cash-neutral (cash 6.78B vs debt 6.90B) but D/E 33.45 (<50) AND current ratio 1.45 (>1.2) — passes.

dividend.detail: Yield 1.7% (<2% so not pass) with payout 28.3% (<90%) — borderline = 0.5.

institutional.detail: Institutions 92.27% (>75%), rec_mean 1.59 (<2.5); 28 of 34 buy/strong-buy with only 1 sell.

ai_exposure.detail: Intuit Assist embeds generative AI across TurboTax, QuickBooks and Credit Karma to lift conversion and ARPU; its data moat (tax/financial records) favors incumbency. Long-tail threat from AI-native tax/bookkeeping disruptors exists but is secondary near-term.

deal_radar.assessment: Short interest benign at 4.36% of float, 2.02 days to cover, roughly flat MoM (+3.2%) — no meaningful bear positioning despite the 65% drawdown, consistent with a multiple-reset rather than a fundamental-break story.

catalysts_general: 39% discount to the implied PE from the 460 median target; high target 921.
Forward PE 10.31 vs trailing 17.18 — a high-margin compounder (op margin 47.0%, ROE 22.5%) at a market-like multiple.
65% drawdown is the steepest of the six despite revenue +10.4% and earnings +10.7%.
Modest, well-covered dividend (1.7% yield, 28% payout) plus 6.8% FCF yield.

downside_scenarios.scenario: Retest of the 52w low (268.01) on tax-season cyclicality fears.
AI-disruption narrative pushes the franchise to a 9x trough multiple.

ideal_entry.reasoning: Accumulate near the 268 floor where a 47%-operating-margin franchise prints ~10x forward earnings — historically a deep-value entry for INTU; size up only if it holds the low.

comparables.note: Not in input; SMB-services peer trades at ~2.5x INTU's forward multiple.
Not in input; payroll/SMB comp far more expensive on slower growth.

value_picks.analyst_take: A best-in-class 47% operating-margin franchise marked down 65% to ~10x forward earnings, a 39% discount to consensus, and 28 of 34 analysts bullish. The AI-tax-disruption fear created the gap; the FCF and data moat argue it's overdone. Score 5.0/6 — BUY into the 270 floor.

value_picks.name: Zoetis Inc.

pe_discount.detail: Fwd PE 10.77 vs implied PE 17.51 (median target 130 / fwd EPS 7.42) = 38.5% discount, >30%.

balance_sheet.detail: D/E 286.24 (>150) triggers fail; net debt of 7.31B (cash 1.94B vs debt 9.25B). Offsetting positive: current ratio 3.15.

dividend.detail: Yield 2.65% (>2%) AND payout 33.3% (<70%) — clean pass, the only full dividend pass in the group.

institutional.detail: Institutions 102.9% (>75%), rec_mean 2.05 (<2.5); 11 of 20 buy, no sells.

ai_exposure.detail: Animal health (companion + livestock). AI aids diagnostics and R&D at the margin, but demand is driven by pet ownership and protein consumption, not technology cycles. Neither a tailwind nor a disruption risk of consequence.

deal_radar.assessment: Short interest light at 3.63% of float (1.45 days to cover) and falling MoM (-9.0%) — bears covering into the drawdown, a mild positive tell.

catalysts_general: 39% discount to the implied PE from the 130 median target.
Highest profitability quality in the group: ROE 67.7%, op margin 36.6%, profit margin 28.0%.
Secure, growing dividend (2.65% yield, 33% payout).
Caveat: growth has decelerated (revenue +2.9%, earnings +6.0%) and leverage is heavy (D/E 286).

downside_scenarios.scenario: Retest of 52w low on continued growth deceleration.
Multiple compresses further if competitive/parasiticide pricing pressure persists.

ideal_entry.reasoning: Buy near the 72.38 low where a 68%-ROE franchise yields 2.65% at ~10x forward earnings; the leverage warrants a tighter size than the net-cash names.

comparables.note: Not in input; companion-animal diagnostics peer at ~3x ZTS's multiple.
Not in input; closest animal-health competitor, similar multiple but lower margins/ROE.

value_picks.analyst_take: High-quality, defensive animal-health franchise (ROE 68%) cut in half to ~11x forward with a secure 2.65% yield and 39% discount to target. The two cautions are decelerating growth (rev +2.9%) and a leveraged balance sheet (D/E 286, the only criterion it fails). Score 4.5/6 — BUY but size for the leverage.

value_picks.name: ServiceNow, Inc.

fcf_yield.detail: FCF 5.108B / mcap 107.41B = 4.76%, low end of the 4-7% band.

pe_discount.detail: Fwd PE 20.72 vs implied PE 27.35 (median target 137.50 / fwd EPS 5.03) = 24.3% discount, in the 15-30% band.

balance_sheet.detail: Net cash: cash 5.18B > debt 2.43B (+2.75B). Pass on net-cash condition despite current ratio 0.85.

institutional.detail: Institutions 88.5% (>75%), rec_mean 1.44 (<2.5); 43 of 48 buy/strong-buy — strongest analyst conviction in the group.

ai_exposure.detail: Now Assist / agentic AI is a genuine upsell lever across IT, security and customer workflows, and ServiceNow is positioned as a platform beneficiary of enterprise AI adoption. The risk is that this is already embedded in a 20.7x forward / 7.7x sales valuation.

deal_radar.assessment: Short interest rising — 5.93% of float, +24.0% MoM (49.2M -> 61.0M shares) — the largest proportional short build in the group, though only 2.05 days to cover. Worth watching as a sentiment shift against the still-rich multiple.

catalysts_general: Fastest top-line grower of the value names (revenue +22.1%) with a net-cash sheet.
Only a 24% discount to implied target PE and the richest absolute multiple here (fwd PE 20.7, P/S 7.69, P/B 9.16).
Trailing PE 62.0 vs forward 20.7 implies large EPS expansion (fwd EPS 5.03 vs trailing 1.68).
Current ratio 0.85 (<1) is the weakest near-term liquidity in the group, though offset by net cash.

downside_scenarios.scenario: Pullback toward the rising SMA50 (99.4) and below on a growth-multiple wobble.
Retest of 52w low if AI-monetization disappoints expectations.

ideal_entry.reasoning: Wait for the 81-90 zone (near 52w low) before paying for the growth — at 20.7x forward today the value-framework margin of safety is thin despite the headline drawdown.

comparables.note: In input; half NOW's forward multiple though growing slower — illustrates how much premium NOW still carries.
Not in input; comparable platform-SaaS multiple, slower growth than NOW.

value_picks.analyst_take: Highest-quality grower (rev +22%) with overwhelming analyst support (43 of 48 buy) and net cash — but on a value framework it's the most expensive name here at 20.7x forward and 7.7x sales, with the smallest discount to target and a rising short. The drawdown alone doesn't make it cheap. Score 4.5/6 — WATCHLIST pending a sub-90 entry.

value_picks.name: Palantir Technologies Inc.

fcf_yield.detail: FCF 1.753B / mcap 322.94B = 0.54%, far below the 4% floor — by far the worst FCF yield in the group.

pe_discount.detail: Mechanically passes: fwd PE 64.75 vs implied PE 96.14 (median target 200 / fwd EPS 2.08) = 32.6% discount. But note the implied PE itself is a nosebleed 96x — the 'discount' is off an extreme base.

drawdown.detail: Down 35.1% from 52w high of 207.52 — clears 35% by a hair (shallowest drawdown of the six).

balance_sheet.detail: Strong net cash: cash 8.03B vs debt 0.21B (+7.81B); current ratio 6.91 — pristine sheet.

institutional.detail: Institutions 62.4% (>60% but <75%, lowest here), rec_mean 1.90 (<3.0) — borderline, not pass; note 2 sell/strong-sell ratings.

ai_exposure.detail: AIP is a leading enterprise-AI platform and revenue is compounding +84.7% — AI is unambiguously a business tailwind. The problem is valuation, not the franchise: the AI optionality is already priced many times over.

deal_radar.assessment: Short interest modest at 3.23% of float (1.58 days) but building (+16.8% MoM). The notable tell is the widest analyst dispersion in the group — target low 70 vs high 255 and 2 sell-side bears — signaling unusually low consensus on fair value.

catalysts_general: Hyper-growth: revenue +84.7%, earnings +325%, margins elite (gross 84%, op 46%, net 44%).
But valuation is extreme on every value metric: P/S 61.8x, P/B 38.2x, trailing PE 151, forward PE 64.75.
0.54% FCF yield means a value buyer is paying for ~a decade-plus of growth up front.
Lowest institutional ownership (62%) and most polarized analyst targets (70 to 255).

downside_scenarios.scenario: Retest of 52w low — still 59x forward, i.e. not cheap even at the low.
Move to the analyst low target; even a ~48% drop only brings it to 34x forward.

ideal_entry.reasoning: No value-framework entry exists at current multiples; even the most bearish analyst target (70) leaves it at 34x forward. A value buyer simply does not get a margin of safety here — this is a momentum/growth name, not a value name.

comparables.note: Not in input; high-multiple data peer, yet trades at a fraction of PLTR's 61x sales.
Not in input; premium-multiple AI/security comp, still markedly cheaper on sales than PLTR.

value_picks.analyst_take: An exceptional business — 85% revenue growth, 44% net margins, net cash — wearing an impossible valuation: 62x sales, 65x forward earnings, 0.5% FCF yield. The mechanical score (4.0) is flattered by a 'discount' measured off a 96x implied PE and a barely-qualifying drawdown. On a value framework this is AVOID; the franchise is real but offers no margin of safety at these multiples.
